signals.title: USD/JPY — COT Extreme + BOJ Event Risk
signals.text: JPY COT Leveraged Funds net short -80,578 contracts (CFTC, week ending 2026-04-21) — near historic extreme; crowded short creates asymmetric squeeze risk. BOJ Policy Rate + Monetary Policy Statement + Outlook Report at 02:30 UTC Apr 28: hotter-than-expected Japan CPI elevates probability of hawkish surprise. USD/JPY at 159.24 — 160.00 is the prior BOJ verbal intervention threshold; a sustained break above 160 historically triggered warning statements. Carry dominates at 400bp Fed-BoJ differential while VIX 18.71 remains below risk-off threshold; COT unwind accelerates on any hawkish MPS language.

signals.title: AUD/CAD — COT Structural Divergence + Carry
signals.text: AUD COT Leveraged Funds net long +47,218 contracts vs CAD COT net short -64,786 contracts (CFTC, week ending 2026-04-21) — widest G8 bilateral COT divergence on the board. RBA 3.35% vs BoC 2.75% adds 60bp carry premium to AUD. COT divergence of 112,004 contracts combined confirms institutional flow alignment. Gold $4,742 provides a secondary AUD tailwind via commodity correlation. CAD extreme short is the squeeze risk — any BoC pivot language compresses the spread. Watch AUD/CAD 0.9040 as near-term pivot.

signals.title: GBP/CHF — CB Differential + Geopolitical Risk Premium
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs SNB 0.00% — 375bp is the widest carry differential in the G8 matrix, structurally bid for GBP/CHF. GBP COT LF net long +33,517 confirms institutional flow aligned with carry. CHF 10Y yield at 0.40%; SNB on hold with deflation risk (CPI 0.10%) eliminates near-term SNB rate support. Bab al-Mandab maritime attack and Iran tensions introduce geopolitical CHF safe-haven demand — but VIX 18.71 sub-threshold limits safe-haven flows to marginal. Carry dominates; geopolitical escalation to VIX > 25 is the key invalidation.

signals.title: Gold — Geopolitical Premium Compression
signals.text: Gold $4,742.1 — stalled Iran peace talks weigh on safe-haven bid; Bab al-Mandab maritime attack introduces a secondary geopolitical channel. VIX 18.71 confirms cross-asset stress remains contained. SPX +0.80% and risk-on posture reduce portfolio gold allocation pressure. AUD and NZD carry a positive correlation to gold at these levels — AUD/USD softness (Middle East tensions) partially offsets the commodity FX bid. Watch $4,700 as the near-term support; a sustained break would remove the remaining geopolitical premium from commodity FX.
